author: felixleopold
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Execute code inside your notes: Shiki syntax highlighting with 65+ themes, live streaming output, inline Matplotlib and Plotly graphs, shared variables, and styled HTML and PDF export with outputs.

manifest.author: Felix Leopold
manifest.description: Execute code inside your notes: Shiki syntax highlighting with 65+ themes, live streaming output, inline Matplotlib and Plotly graphs, shared variables, and styled HTML and PDF export with outputs.
manifest.name: CodeSuite

name: CodeSuite

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Execution and output-panel fixes, plus a cancellable Run All.

## What's New

- **Cancel Run All** — clicking the Run All header button again while a pass is running now stops the current block and ends the pass. The button turns into a stop control while active.

## Bug Fixes

- **Re-running a block after Run All no longer throws** — the shared-context replay used to drop the block's own earlier run, so re-running an early block replayed later blocks first and hit a `NameError` for variables that block defines. The full accumulated session is now replayed in order.
- **No more trailing blank line in output** — the internal variable-snapshot marker printed a spacer newline that surfaced as an empty line after a run.
- **Output-less runs read as "ran fine"** — a successful block with no output now collapses to a slim `Output (none)` header instead of showing an empty output box.

## Upgrade Notes

- No settings or behavior changes beyond the fixes above.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Documentation release — a reworked README and store listing to make the plugin's features easier to discover. No functional changes.

## What's New

- **Hero demo GIF** at the top of the README showing theme switching, live execution, and inline `$var` substitution in one clip.
- **"At a glance" feature table** — the seven headline features (highlighting, execution, inline graphs, notebook variables, embedded files, Jupyter import/export, HTML/PDF export) summarized up front.
- **Restructured README** into four feature pillars — Highlight, Run, Embed, Share — with deeper detail tucked into collapsible sections so the page is easier to scan.
- **Rewritten store description** that leads with code execution and surfaces the Jupyter import/export and styled HTML/PDF export.

## Upgrade Notes

- No behavior changes. Documentation and listing only.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Maintenance release — lint compliance fixes for the Obsidian plugin review.

## Bug Fixes

- **Lint compliance** — replaced three `obsidianmd/ui/sentence-case` rule disables with an `ignoreRegex` allowlist entry for the "Jupyter" and "A4" proper nouns, since disabling that rule is not permitted by the Obsidian reviewer.
- **Dropped a CSS `!important`** — the native Live Preview code-block flair is now suppressed by raising selector specificity instead of `!important`.

## Upgrade Notes

- No behavior changes. Internal lint cleanup only.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Quality and reliability release — multi-language Jupyter round-trips, cancellation polish, skip-badge fixes, and fence-attribute isolation.

## What's New

- **Multi-language Jupyter notebook export** ([#5](https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/issues/5)) — every executable code block now becomes a code cell (not just blocks in the note's dominant language). Blocks in a non-dominant language (e.g. a JavaScript block in a Python notebook) carry a `metadata.vscode.languageId` tag so VS Code renders each cell in the correct language and round-trips back to the right fence on re-import. Notebook import similarly respects per-cell language metadata on cells that carry it.

## Bug Fixes

- **Stopped runs are labelled cleanly** ([#25](https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/issues/25)) — clicking Stop shows *Output (stopped)* when there is partial output, or removes the panel entirely if nothing was produced. Stopped runs are not persisted to the shared session. Run All now also halts when a block is manually stopped.
- **Queued cancel is immediate** ([#25](https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/issues/25)) — cancelling a *Queued* block reverts the pill right away instead of waiting for the prior run to finish.
- **Skip tags show up correctly** ([#15](https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/issues/15)) — skip-badge sync now matches blocks by code hash instead of source position, so blocks that are virtualized off screen no longer shift the skip state onto the wrong blocks.
- **Fence attributes no longer bleed across blocks** — a one-line guard now slices `sectionInfo.text` to the current section, fixing a bug where blocks in Reading view could inherit the `skip`, `preview`, or other flags of the first fence in the note.
- **Output panels cleared properly** — clearing a note's session now also removes live output panels already on screen and reverts any in-progress Run pills, not just the in-memory snapshot cache.
- **Matplotlib style default corrected** — the default is now blank (Matplotlib's stock look) as documented. The 1.8.0 build shipped with `dark_background` as the default by mistake.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes except the Matplotlib style correction: if you installed 1.8.0 and your plots look different after upgrading, the `dark_background` style that was incorrectly defaulted has been cleared. To restore it, set **Settings → Python → Matplotlib style** to `dark_background`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Full code-block chrome in Live Preview — header, Run/Copy, live output, line numbers, collapse, and file embeds now render in the editor, not just Reading view.

## What's New

- **Full Live Preview chrome** — fenced code blocks in Live Preview now render the same header, Run/Copy buttons, Shiki-highlighted body, line numbers, and collapse toggle as Reading view. No more plain, unstyled blocks in the editor.
- **Live Preview file embeds** — `![[file.py]]` embeds on their own line render the full `ocode-wrapper` chrome (with filename link and Run button) in Live Preview, matching Reading view.
- **Edit-on-click reveal** — clicking the code body moves the cursor into the block and reveals its raw source for editing. Moving the cursor out re-renders the chrome instantly.
- **Streaming output survives cursor movement** — a per-block DOM cache returns the *same* wrapper node across cursor in/out cycles, so running output and active processes are never interrupted by moving the cursor.
- **Gutter continuity during reveal** — when line numbers are enabled, a CSS counter gutter mirrors the chrome numbering on the raw HyperMD lines visible while a block is being edited, eliminating the gutter-flash on cursor entry.

## Bug Fixes

- Suppressed Obsidian's native `code-block-flair` widget (the language label + copy button injected at the top-right of every fence in the editor) — it was flashing in the corner the instant the cursor entered a block and our chrome widget was dropped.
- `Run All` and skip-state sync now scope to `.markdown-reading-view` so Live Preview block widgets (which have their own Run buttons and build-time skip state) are never double-counted.
- Line numbers toggle in Settings now applies `ocode-lp-lnum` to `document.body` immediately, without requiring a plugin reload.

## Upgrade Notes

- No settings migration required. The Live Preview chrome uses the same settings as Reading view (line numbers, collapse, execution, theme). Existing notes open and render without any user action.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
A code-quality release that clears all outstanding Obsidian plugin reviewer warnings with no change to behavior.

## What's New

- Cleared every source and CSS warning reported by the Obsidian plugin reviewer: timer and animation-frame calls now use `window` (instead of the `activeWindow.*` and bare-global forms), Node's `require` is reached through `window` instead of `globalThis`, and intentionally-ignored errors use optional `catch` bindings rather than unused `_e`/`_err` variables.
- Reworked the soft-wrap hang-indent so it no longer relies on the `:has()` selector (broad style invalidation) or `text-indent` (only partially supported in older Obsidian). Wrapped code lines still align exactly with the code column via a renderer marker class and an absolutely-positioned line number.

## Bug Fixes

- None — this release is behavior-preserving cleanup.

## Upgrade Notes

- No action required. No settings, data, or rendering behavior changes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Typed variable injection (fixing #16), plus an experimental way to use markdown tables as code variables.

## What's New

- **Typed `vars` injection** ([#16](https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/issues/16)): values in `vars` blocks and `code_vars:` frontmatter are now injected as native, correctly-typed literals. `5` is an `int`, `True` is a `bool`, `0.85` is a `float`, and a quoted `"https://x"` is a clean string — no more manual casting or double-quoting.
- **Live cross-language variables**: a shared variable changed by one block is now visible to later blocks in *any* language, in execution order. Set `count = 42` in Python and a later Bash block sees `42`; change it in Bash and the next Python block sees the new value (re-typed). Re-running a block no longer double-applies its own assignment. **Apply** on a `vars` block (or **Clear Session**) resets shared vars to their declared values.
- **`:type` hints**: force a type with `port:str = 8080`, `ratio:float = 1`, or `ids:json = [1, 2, 3]`. Hints: `str`, `int`, `float`, `bool`, `json`.
- **Multiline string vars**: triple-quoted (`"""` or `'''`) values can span multiple lines.
- **Data tables (experimental)** — Settings → Experimental features → Data tables (off by default): a `%% codesuite: <name> as <shape> %%` directive above a markdown table exposes it to code as `records`, `dict`, `columns`, `matrix`, or `vars`. A `var | value` header is recognised automatically.

## Bug Fixes

- `vars` string values are no longer wrapped in an extra layer of quotes (e.g. `BASE_URL` was becoming `'"https://…"'`, breaking URL navigation).
- Integers and booleans from `vars` blocks are no longer injected as strings, so comparisons like `if depth > CRAWL_DEPTH:` work without manual `int()`/`bool()` casting.

## Upgrade Notes

- Existing `vars` blocks keep working. If your code relied on every value being a string and you previously cast it (e.g. `int(MY_INT)`), those casts are now redundant but harmless. Add a `:str` hint if you specifically need a value kept as a string.
- Shared variables are now a live cross-language namespace updated in execution order. If you relied on a `vars` value always resetting to its declared value on every run regardless of prior runs, use **Apply** / **Clear Session** to reset, or keep that variable read-only.
- Data tables are experimental and off by default; their syntax may change in a future release.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Soft-wrap support for reading view, hideable clear-session button, and extra blank line fix.

## What's New

- **Soft-wrap long lines** (Settings → Appearance): code blocks in reading view now wrap long lines instead of showing a horizontal scrollbar, matching the editor's behaviour. On by default.
- **Hideable clear-session button** (Settings → Code execution): the "Clear execution session" button in the tab bar can now be turned off to declutter the header. The command palette entry is always available. The button is never shown on mobile.

## Bug Fixes

- Removed the extra blank line that appeared at the bottom of every code block in reading view. Every block was rendering one line taller than its actual content.

## Upgrade Notes

- Soft-wrap is on by default. If you prefer horizontal scrolling, disable it under Settings → Appearance → Soft-wrap long lines.
- The clear-session button now only appears on desktop (it was always a no-op on mobile). No action needed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fixes the line count that was missing from most code blocks.

## Bug Fixes

- Line count ("N lines") now appears in the header of every code block, not only when the collapsible setting is enabled. The count was always tied to the collapse toggle, which is off by default, making it effectively invisible for most users.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release makes shell execution explicit and predictable: you can now pin the exact bash, zsh, and sh binaries, the `sh` fence no longer secretly runs bash, and the line counter is fixed.

## What's New

- New **Bash path**, **Zsh path**, and **Shell (sh) path** settings under Settings → CodeSuite → Environment. Each lets you pin the exact interpreter for that language; leave empty to keep the current PATH-resolved behavior.
- To restore the old `obsidian-execute-code` behavior where `shell` blocks ran under modern bash, set **Shell (sh) path** to `/opt/homebrew/bin/bash` (Apple Silicon) or `/usr/local/bin/bash` (Intel).

## Bug Fixes

- Line-count hint is no longer off by one — a one-line block now reads "1 line" instead of "2 lines", with correct singular/plural wording.

## Breaking Changes

- `sh` code blocks now run **POSIX sh** (`/bin/sh`), matching `shell` blocks. Previously `sh` was aliased to bash. `bash`, `zsh`, and `shell` blocks are unchanged.
- If you have `sh` blocks that rely on bash features (arrays, `[[ ]]`, etc.), either rename the fence to `bash`, or set **Shell (sh) path** to a bash binary.
- A one-time notice explaining this appears after you update; it will not show again.

## Upgrade Notes

- Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin after updating.
- Pointing **Zsh path** at a non-zsh binary (e.g. bash) will fail — variable tracking emits zsh-specific syntax. Keep each path on its matching shell family.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release adds the requested PHP, PowerShell, and shell startup execution improvements, and fixes one remaining skip-badge alignment bug when notes include a `vars` block.

## What's New

- PHP snippets that omit `<?php` now run by default; CodeSuite adds the tag only to the temporary execution file.
- PowerShell code blocks and `.ps1` files can run through `pwsh` when PowerShell 7+ is installed.
- Bash and Zsh blocks can run in login mode, and Bash/Zsh/Shell blocks can source one or more configured startup files before execution.
- Skip badges now stay attached to the correct executable block even when a note also contains a `vars` block.

## Bug Fixes

- Exclude `vars` blocks from the live skip-badge indexing pass so badges line up with the same executable block order used by Run All.
- Mark only inline executable code blocks as source-aligned skip targets, preventing helper blocks from shifting badge placement.
- Add a Zsh-native shared-variable snapshotter so Zsh blocks can update inline `$varname` references without using Bash-only `compgen`.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin after updating.
- The PHP opening-tag behavior is enabled by default and can be disabled in Settings → CodeSuite → Environment.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release fixes one remaining skip-badge alignment bug when notes include a `vars` block.

## What's New

- Skip badges now stay attached to the correct executable block even when a note also contains a `vars` block.

## Bug Fixes

- Exclude `vars` blocks from the live skip-badge indexing pass so badges line up with the same executable block order used by Run All.
- Mark only inline executable code blocks as source-aligned skip targets, preventing helper blocks from shifting badge placement.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin after updating.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release replaces the clunky preview-rerender skip badge fix with a simpler render-time solution that behaves like normal code block language updates.

## What's New

- Skip badges now rely on Obsidian's normal markdown post-processing during reading-mode render, matching how language changes appear without forcing a preview rerender (#15).

## Bug Fixes

- Removed the forced `previewMode.rerender(true)` call on layout changes, which could make reading mode jump or feel unstable.
- Keep the lightweight save/edit DOM sync for already-rendered views without rebuilding the markdown preview.
- Keep the corrected fence-attribute parser so initial reading-mode renders pick up `skip`, `collapsed`, and `expanded` attributes reliably (#15).

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin after updating to remove the old layout-change rerender listener.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release fixes macOS command lookup and finishes the skip-state refresh work so code block headers stay accurate across edits, saves, and reading-mode switches.

## What's New

- Skip badges now refresh after edit-to-reading-mode transitions, so preview headers reflect the current `skip` state immediately (#15).
- Run All now reads the live in-memory note content, so unsaved `skip` changes are honored without reopening the note (#15).

## Bug Fixes

- Prepend common Homebrew paths on macOS so `brew`, `node`, `python`, and similar tools work from Obsidian-launched shells.
- Re-sync skip badges on saves, debounced editor changes, and preview layout rebuilds to eliminate stale header state (#15).
- Fix fenced-block skip indexing so already-running blocks no longer shift later skip states during Run All (#15).
- Parse fence attributes from the current rendered section so preview-mode `skip` badges are reliable on initial render (#15).
- Color stderr orange by default and only switch to red when the process exits with a non-zero status.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Reload Obsidian or toggle the plugin after updating to activate the new preview-sync listeners.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release fixes the `skip` tag not being picked up immediately and improves the import-as-alias workflow.

## Bug Fixes
- **Skip tag now works immediately** — `# codesuite:skip` and the fence `skip` attribute are now read from the current file source when Run All is triggered, so you no longer need to close and reopen the note for changes to take effect (#15)
- **Import alias sidebar refresh** — the file explorer now updates as soon as Obsidian indexes the new symlink (uses the vault `create` event instead of a fixed 250 ms timeout), making the file appear instantly after import

## What's New
- **Import alias opens in a new tab** — after a successful *Import code file as alias…*, the file is opened in a new tab rather than replacing the current one

## Upgrade Notes
- No breaking changes. Existing settings are preserved.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This patch release tightens up the new code-file view without changing its workflow. Embedded/code-file headers stay clickable for collapse, alignment is restored, and the editor CSS now passes the previous release's lint warnings without relying on `!important` overrides.

## What's New

- No new user-facing features in this release.

## Bug Fixes

- Restored left alignment for collapsible code-file and embedded-file blocks by removing the extra outer gutter wrapper while keeping header-click collapse behavior.
- Removed the code-file editor's `!important` CSS overrides by scoping selectors more precisely, which resolves the reported CSS lint warnings without changing editor appearance.
- Cleaned up an unused empty CSS ruleset in the output pill styles.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Existing settings and note content continue to work as-is.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Major feature release: code files are now first-class citizens of the vault (editor + Run + symlink imports), the notebook surface gets `.env` files, `code_vars:` frontmatter and a `codesuite:skip` marker for Run All, and every successful run gets a one-click **Copy output** button.

## What's New

- **Code files in the file explorer** (#4, #11) — `.py`, `.js`, `.ts`, `.sh`, `.go`, `.rb`, `.rs`, `.cpp`, `.swift`, `.lua`, and the rest of CodeSuite's supported extensions now register with Obsidian and open in a lightweight in-vault editor with syntax-highlighted preview, plain-text edit mode, autosave, and a **Run** button with live streaming output.
- **Import code file as alias…** (#14) — new command palette entry that opens a native file picker and symlinks the chosen external file into your vault under the configured Imports folder (default: `CodeSuiteImports/`). Edits write through to the real file on disk.
- **Copy output button** (#6) — every successful run shows a **Copy output** pill next to the Clear button.
- **Collapsible inline code blocks** (#7) — opt-in toggle in settings adds a collapse/expand header to inline code blocks in Reading view, with an optional "collapsed by default" mode.
- **`.env` file support** (#8) — point CodeSuite at any `.env` file on disk; variables are loaded into every executed block. Per-vault overrides in **Extra environment variables** still win.
- **`codesuite:skip` marker for Run All** (#9) — add `# codesuite:skip` (or `//`, `--`, `%`, `/* … */`) as the first line of any block to keep Run All from executing it. Skipped blocks show a small **skip** badge in their toolbar and can still be run individually.
- **`code_vars:` frontmatter** (#10) — declare shared note variables in YAML frontmatter; values flow into inline `` `$varname` `` substitutions and executable blocks just like a `vars` block. A `vars` block still wins when both define the same key.

## Bug Fixes

- None in this release — see issue tracker for ongoing work.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. All new settings ship with safe defaults.
- **Show code files in the file explorer** is enabled by default; restart Obsidian once after upgrading so the extension registration takes effect.
- Extensions already claimed by Obsidian or another plugin (`.md`, `.json`, `.css`, `.html`, `.xml`) are skipped automatically.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Major feature release: code files are now first-class citizens of the vault (editor + Run + symlink imports), the notebook surface gets `.env` files, `code_vars:` frontmatter and a `codesuite:skip` marker for Run All, and every successful run gets a one-click **Copy output** button.

## What's New

- **Code files in the file explorer** (#4, #11) — `.py`, `.js`, `.ts`, `.sh`, `.go`, `.rb`, `.rs`, `.cpp`, `.swift`, `.lua`, and the rest of CodeSuite's supported extensions now register with Obsidian and open in a lightweight in-vault editor with syntax-highlighted preview, plain-text edit mode, autosave, and a **Run** button with live streaming output.
- **Import code file as alias…** (#14) — new command palette entry that opens a native file picker and symlinks the chosen external file into your vault under the configured Imports folder (default: `CodeSuiteImports/`). Edits write through to the real file on disk.
- **Copy output button** (#6) — every successful run shows a **Copy output** pill next to the Clear button.
- **Collapsible inline code blocks** (#7) — opt-in toggle in settings adds a collapse/expand header to inline code blocks in Reading view, with an optional "collapsed by default" mode.
- **`.env` file support** (#8) — point CodeSuite at any `.env` file on disk; variables are loaded into every executed block. Per-vault overrides in **Extra environment variables** still win.
- **`codesuite:skip` marker for Run All** (#9) — add `# codesuite:skip` (or `//`, `--`, `%`, `/* … */`) as the first line of any block to keep Run All from executing it. Skipped blocks show a small **skip** badge in their toolbar and can still be run individually.
- **`code_vars:` frontmatter** (#10) — declare shared note variables in YAML frontmatter; values flow into inline `` `$varname` `` substitutions and executable blocks just like a `vars` block. A `vars` block still wins when both define the same key.

## Bug Fixes

## Upgrade Notes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
This release ships three bug fixes that improve Bash execution reliability, env variable handling, and embedded file rendering, plus a full documentation overhaul with a rewritten README, new configuration reference, marketplace listing doc, and SECURITY.md.

## What's New

- Added GitHub issue templates (bug report and feature request forms)
- Added `docs/marketplace-listing.md` — tracks the Obsidian plugin listing "About" text and short description in one place
- Added `docs/configuration.md` — full settings reference (the README now links here instead of repeating everything inline)
- Added `SECURITY.md` — private vulnerability reporting instructions and scope notes
- Expanded `CONTRIBUTING.md` with an internal architecture overview ("How it works")
- README rewritten: shorter, less repetitive, visual-first structure with GIF placeholder slots for upcoming demo recordings
- Planned Upgrades roadmap expanded to 10 items, each linked to its GitHub issue

## Bug Fixes

- Fixed Bash shared-context execution: replaced `{ } > /dev/null 2>&1` preamble with exec-based fd swapping (`exec 3>&1 4>&2 1>/dev/null 2>&1` … `exec 1>&3 2>&4`), which correctly handles function definitions with complex bodies and heredocs
- Fixed env variable parser stripping surrounding quotes from values (e.g. `KEY="value"` and `KEY='value'` now correctly yield `value` instead of `"value"`)
- Fixed embedded code file rendering to pass `sourcePath` through to `renderCodeBlock`, resolving potential link resolution issues for embedded files

## Upgrade Notes

No breaking changes. Existing settings are preserved.

releases.author: felixleopold
releases.description:
## What's Fixed

### Plugin Description Character Limit
- Reduced manifest.json description to comply with Obsidian Community Plugins 250-character limit
- Maintains all searchable keywords and core feature highlights
- Ensures smooth submission and listing

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/compare/1.2.3...1.2.4
releases.name: 1.2.4: Plugin Description Compliance

releases.author: felixleopold
releases.description:
## What's New

### Comprehensive README Redesign
- **Magnetic tagline** — all core features in one searchable sentence
- **Detailed feature descriptions** — Syntax Highlighting, Code Execution with 13 runtimes, Shared Execution Context, Embedded Files
- **Complete setup guide** — walkthrough of every setting, examples for venv paths, virtualenv activation, custom themes, and environment variables
- **Expanded capabilities** — now clearly documents:
  - All 13 supported runtimes (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Bash, Zsh, Shell, Go, Ruby, Lua, Perl, R, PHP, Swift)
  - Virtual environment support and automatic activation
  - Matplotlib & Plotly inline graph rendering
  - Interactive stdin with password masking for `sudo` prompts
  - Inline `$varname` substitution and shared state across blocks
  - Auto light/dark theme switching

### Marketing Copy Optimization
- Jupyter Notebook comparison positioned upfront
- Zero-setup, in-vault execution emphasized
- Keywords optimized for search discoverability while maintaining readability

### Version Bumps
- `manifest.json` version: 1.2.2 → 1.2.3
- `manifest.json` description updated to new tagline
- `package.json` version: 1.2.2 → 1.2.3

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/felixleopold/obsidian-code-suite/compare/1.2.2...1.2.3
releases.name: 1.2.3: Enhanced Documentation & Better Plugin Branding

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Fixed

### Apache 2.0 license now correctly detected by GitHub
- Replaced LICENSE file with the exact canonical text from apache.org
- Previous file was missing the APPENDIX section (20 lines) and had incorrect word-wrap line breaks in the main body
- GitHub's licensee uses fuzzy matching with a high similarity threshold — the malformed file fell below it
- package.json license field also updated to SPDX identifier Apache-2.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's Fixed

### `vars` blocks now use `ini` syntax highlighting
- Variables are now displayed with proper `ini` formatting: `key = value` (unquoted)
- Previously highlighted as Python strings with fake double-quote wrapping (`key = "value"`)
- Better readability and more idiomatic representation

### Run All stops immediately on block errors
- When a block exits with a non-zero status or times out, Run All now halts immediately
- Shows a notice with the error and block number
- Prevents cascading errors and saves time when a block fails
- Cumulative execution context is preserved up to the failed block

### Duplicate view-header buttons fixed
- Run All and Clear Session buttons in the view header no longer duplicate on plugin reload
- Buttons are properly cleaned up on plugin unload and checked before re-adding
- Fixed memory leak in button tracking with proper DOM cleanup

### Apache 2.0 license now correctly detected by GitHub
- Updated `package.json` to use SPDX identifier `"Apache-2.0"`
- GitHub's license detection now correctly identifies the project as Apache 2.0 licensed
- Previously conflicted with LICENSE file due to incorrect `package.json` entry

### README improvements
- Cleaner, less repetitive feature descriptions
- Added missing features: `vars` blocks, Run All, Clear Session, password masking
- Added GitHub issues link for bug reports and feature requests
- Better organization of content

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adds "Run All" and "Clear Session" buttons to the top-right of every note view, and switches the license to Apache 2.0.

## What's New

- **Run All button** (▶ play-circle icon) in the note view header — runs every executable code block in the note from top to bottom, waiting for each to finish before starting the next so shared execution context accumulates in the correct order. Shows a notice if no blocks are found (switch to Reading view first).
- **Clear Session button** (↩ rotate-ccw icon) in the note view header — replaces the per-output "Clear session" pill. Always accessible, even before running any code.

## Bug Fixes / Changes

- Removed the "Clear session" pill that previously appeared inside each output panel, reducing clutter.

## License

- Switched from MIT to **Apache License 2.0**. Copyright remains with Felix Leopold. Apache 2.0 requires attribution in derivative works and includes an explicit patent grant.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Existing settings and sessions are preserved.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adds Copy and Apply buttons to `vars` blocks.

## What's New

- **Copy button** on `vars` blocks — copies the raw variable definitions to the clipboard, consistent with regular code blocks.
- **Apply button** on `vars` blocks — re-seeds all variables into the session without needing to close and reopen the note. Useful after clearing a session with the "Clear session" button.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Existing notes and settings are preserved.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Adds shared execution context with working inline `$var` substitution for Python and Bash, a new `vars` fenced block for declaring note-scoped variables, and several bug fixes.

## What's New

- **`vars` fenced block** — declare note-scoped variables without running any code. Rendered as a styled key-value table; values are immediately available for `$varname` substitution in the note.
- **Bash variable extraction** — user-defined scalar variables from `bash`/`zsh`/`sh` blocks are now captured and injected into the shared variable store, so `$varname` substitution works for shell blocks just like Python.
- **Shared context on by default** — new vaults start with `Shared execution context` enabled; existing installs can toggle it in settings.

## Bug Fixes

- **Inline `$varname` always appeared dim** — `data-resolved` attribute was never being set, so the "resolved" CSS rule never applied. Fixed: both `updateInlineVarRefs` and `processInlineVarRefs` now set the attribute when a value is applied.
- **"Clear session" didn't reset inline var displays** — clearing a note's session now queries all `$varname` spans in the DOM and resets their text and styling to the unresolved state.
- **matplotlib/plotly plots bleeding into wrong block** — when re-running a block with shared context, accumulated prior-block code was calling `plt.show()` (which saves image files), causing the image to appear in the current block's output. Fixed by patching both `matplotlib` and `plotly.io` show functions to no-ops during context replay.
- **Bash var postamble leaked shell internals** — the first implementation used short variable names that showed up in the captured output. Fixed: all helper locals renamed to `__ocode_*` prefix, and the skip filter extended to cover `EPOCHREALTIME`, `EPOCHSECONDS`, `SRANDOM`, and all `__ocode_*` names.
- **`document.adoptNode` Obsidian rule violation** — replaced bare `document.adoptNode` with `activeDocument.adoptNode` in the code block renderer.

## Upgrade Notes

- No breaking changes. Existing settings are preserved.
- If you previously had `Shared execution context` disabled, it will remain disabled after upgrading. New installs default to enabled.

releases.author: felixleopold
releases.description:
# CodeSuite 1.0.0 — Initial Release

## Features

- **Shiki Syntax Highlighting** — VS Code-quality highlighting in both reading and editing modes
- **65+ Built-in Themes** — Catppuccin, Dracula, GitHub, Gruvbox, Material, Nord, Tokyo Night, and more
- **Custom VS Code Theme Import** — Load any VS Code `.json` theme file
- **Code Execution** — Run code blocks in 11 languages with live streaming output
- **Embedded Code Files** — Render external files as highlighted, collapsible code blocks
- **Smart Stdin Detection** — Input bar appears only when code reads from stdin
- **Dynamic Theme Colors** — UI automatically adapts to the selected syntax theme

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-code-suite/` folder.
